source_logs: rch exec stdout: broader longer-window eight-scenario negative-guardrail publication

methodology.comparison_strategy: same target dir comparator corpus; longer-window baseline, auto, and rollback captures over the eight-scenario dual-policy family
methodology.value_contract: longer-window percentile proxy and interval-endpoint comparison across the same multi-scenario corpus
commands: rch exec -- bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-e5-long-baseline; ASUPERSYNC_GF256_PROFILE_PACK=scalar-conservative-v1 ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_POLICY=never cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- RQ-E-GF256-DUAL --sample-size 40 --warm-up-time 0.2 --measurement-time 0.2'

rch exec -- bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-e5-long-auto; ASUPERSYNC_GF256_PROFILE_PACK=auto ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_POLICY=auto cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- RQ-E-GF256-DUAL --sample-size 40 --warm-up-time 0.2 --measurement-time 0.2'

rch exec -- bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-e5-long-rollback; ASUPERSYNC_GF256_PROFILE_PACK=auto ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_POLICY=auto ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_MUL_MIN_TOTAL=32768 ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_MUL_MAX_TOTAL=32768 cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- RQ-E-GF256-DUAL --sample-size 40 --warm-up-time 0.2 --measurement-time 0.2'

candidate_results.summary: Retains the canonical current x86 default contract while the broader longer-window corpus continues to show overall negative guardrail evidence.
Still rejected as the broader corpus does not support promoting mul auto defaults.

closure_assessment.summary: This successor packet widens Track-E to the eight-scenario RQ-E-GF256-DUAL-001..008 corpus and preserves the current x86 default contract, but the broader longer-window percentile proxies still regress under auto, so the packet remains a negative guardrail rather than closure-grade proof.
blocking_requirements: Publish a closure-grade broader refresh with raw-sample capture or materially better broader interval-proxy evidence across the multi-scenario SIMD-active corpus.
Demonstrate materially better broader-corpus auto tail behavior than the scalar conservative baseline instead of the current regressed percentile proxies.
Keep highconf_v1 as the narrowed negative guardrail and treat this v3 packet as the broader successor guardrail until a new closure-grade artifact/schema version is published.

findings: The broader longer-window corpus widens scope to RQ-E-GF256-DUAL-001..008 while preserving the canonical 2026-03-04 x86 default-selection contract.
Overall broader-corpus percentile proxies regress under auto versus baseline: p50 = 0.5034 us vs 0.3857 us, and p95/p99 = 1.1657 us vs 1.0449 us.
mul_slices2_auto stays slightly favorable on average, but addmul_slices2_auto regresses on six of eight scenarios, so the broader packet remains negative evidence.
limitations: This packet supersedes the broader directional refresh in artifacts/raptorq_track_e_gf256_multiscenario_refresh_v2.json while preserving highconf_v1 as the narrowed closure-status guardrail.
The broader longer-window corpus remains interval-proxy evidence rather than raw-sample closure-grade proof.
Any future positive closure attempt must publish a new artifact/schema version instead of mutating the v2 or v3 guardrail packets in place.
